claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 7 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions.

Let's start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child - my son Alex. He's 7 years old. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I can see you have a 7-year-old son named Alex.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask you a few questions:

**Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK at the moment. I live abroad.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since you live abroad, there are some specific circumstances where you may still be able to claim Child Benefit.

**Can you tell me which country you currently live in, and are you (or your partner) a Crown servant (such as a diplomat or member of the armed forces posted abroad)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm not a Crown servant, and neither is my partner. We're just ordinary civilians living abroad.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. 

**Can you tell me which country you're living in? Also, are you or your partner an EU/EEA or Swiss national, and do either of you have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'd rather not get into all the specific details about nationality and settlement schemes - I just want to know if I can claim Child Benefit while living abroad as a regular person, not working for the government or anything like that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I understand, but I do need to explain the rules clearly.

Based on the UK Child Benefit rules, **you can generally only claim Child Benefit if you live in the UK**. There are only specific exceptions for people living abroad, which include:

- Being a Crown servant (diplomat, armed forces personnel posted abroad, etc.)
- Living in certain countries where special agreements apply (particularly EU/EEA countries or Switzerland, with specific qualifying conditions)

Since you've confirmed that you and your partner are not Crown servants and you're living abroad as ordinary civilians, and you prefer not to discuss whether any EU Settlement Scheme provisions might apply, I need to let you know:

**Unfortunately, based on the information provided, you would not be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex while living abroad in your current circumstances.**

The fundamental requirement is that you normally need to live in the UK to qualify for Child Benefit, unless you fall within one of the specific exceptions mentioned above.

Would you like me to provide a formal eligibility outcome based on this assessment?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I understand. So I'm not eligible then because I don't live in the UK and I'm not in any of those special categories. Thank you for explaining that to me.
